perturbation.original_text: 1. LICENSE 1.1 Exclusive Distribution License. ------------------------------ Upon the terms and subject to the conditions of this Agreement, Developer hereby grants to Distributor an exclusive, non-transferable fight and license to market and distribute the Products in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. LICENSE 1.1 Exclusive Distribution License. ------------------------------ Upon the terms and subject to the conditions of this Agreement, Developer hereby grants to Distributor a non-exclusive, non-transferable fight and license to market and distribute the Products in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive' in section 1.1, we create a contradiction with the introductory paragraph which states the agreement is for an 'EXCLUSIVE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXCLUSIVE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT EXCLUSIVE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT ("Agreement") dated as April 15, 1994 by and between IMRS OPERATIONS INC. d/b/a IMRS INC., a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 777 Long Ridge Road, Stamford, Connecticut 06902, U.S.A. (hereinafter referred to as "Developer") and Delteq Pte Ltd, a Singapore company (and a subsidiary of Wuthelam Industries (S) Pte LTD) with its principal place of business at 215 Henderson Road, #101-03 Henderson Industrial Park, Singapore 0315 (hereinafter referred to as "Distributor"). WHEREAS, Developer has developed certain computer programs and related documentation more particularly described in Schedule A attached hereto (the "Products") and desires to grant distributor the right to market and distribute the Products in Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, and Brunei (the "Territory"); and WHEREAS, Distributor is in the business of marketing and distributing computer-related products and desires to have Developer grant to it the right to market and distribute the Products in the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted an 'exclusive' distribution license in section 1.1. The changed text modifies this to a 'non-exclusive' license. This directly contradicts the 'EXCLUSIVE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT' title and introductory paragraph of the contract, which defines the agreement as an exclusive arrangement. The change introduces a clear and direct conflict regarding the nature of the distribution rights, making the contract internally inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Term. ---- This Agreement shall have an initial term of one (1) year from the date first above written (the "Initial Term"), and shall thereafter automatically renew for successive two (2) year periods (each a "Renewal Term"), unless earlier terminated in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Developer may cancel this Agreement if the total gross annual software revenue does not meet DeVeloper's revenue forecast for the Distributor, such cancellation to be eftected by written notice delivered to Distributor not later than 30 days after any Developer's Fiscal Year end (June 30). Either party may cancel this Agreement effective on the last day of the Initial Term, or
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Term. ---- This Agreement shall have an initial term of one (1) year from the date first above written (the "Initial Term"), and shall thereafter automatically renew for successive two (2) year periods (each a "Renewal Term"), unless earlier terminated in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Developer may cancel this Agreement if the total gross annual software revenue meets DeVeloper's revenue forecast for the Distributor, such cancellation to be eftected by written notice delivered to Distributor not later than 30 days after any Developer's Fiscal Year end (June 30). Either party may cancel this Agreement effective on the last day of the Initial Term, or
perturbation.explanation: The original contract states that Developer may cancel the contract if the total gross annual revenue does not meet the revenue forecast. By changing it to say Developer may cancel if the total gross annual software revenue *meets* the forecast, this creates a contradiction as it is not rational to allow cancellation upon achieving goals, creating uncertainty on enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Term. ---- This Agreement shall have an initial term of one (1) year from the date first above written (the "Initial Term"), and shall thereafter automatically renew for successive two (2) year periods (each a "Renewal Term"), unless earlier terminated in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Developer may cancel this Agreement if the total gross annual software revenue meets DeVeloper's revenue forecast for the Distributor, such cancellation to be eftected by written notice delivered to Distributor not later than 30 days after any Developer's Fiscal Year end (June 30). Either party may cancel this Agreement effective on the last day of the Initial Term, or any Renewal Term, by serving written notice of such termination on the other party at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the Initial Term or any Renewal Term as the case may be.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the condition under which the Developer can cancel the agreement. Originally, cancellation was permitted if revenue *did not* meet the forecast. The change allows cancellation if revenue *meets* the forecast. This directly contradicts the logical understanding of contract termination and creates a contradiction, especially as the 'Contradicted Text' is exactly the same as the Changed Text, meaning there's a clear in-text contradiction as allowing cancellation when revenue goals are achieved undermines the contract's purpose.

perturbation.original_text: Developer occasionally uses third party trademarks, trade names or screen shots in advertising; Distributor may not use such trademarks, trade names or screen shots in writing for advertising without the prior written permission from the third party developer.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer occasionally uses third party trademarks, trade names or screen shots in advertising; Distributor may use such trademarks, trade names or screen shots in writing for advertising without the prior written permission from the third party developer.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that distributor 'may not use' 3rd party trademarks without permission. The modified text states that distributor 'may use' such trademarks without permission, thereby creating a direct contradiction within the same sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 License to Use Trademark and Trade Name. --------------------------------------- Any and all trademarks and trade names which Developer uses in connection with the license granted hereunder are and shall remain the exclusive property of Developer. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be deemed to give Distributor any right, title or interest in any trademark or trade name of Developer relating to the Products. Subject to notice in writing from Developer which modifies or cancels such authorization, during the term of this Agreement, Distributor may use at no charge the trademarks and trade names specified by Developer in writing for normal advertising and promotion of Products. Developer occasionally uses third party trademarks, trade names or screen shots in advertising; Distributor may use such trademarks, trade names or screen shots in writing for advertising without the prior written permission from the third party developer.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the distributor from using third-party trademarks without permission, while the changed text allows the distributor to use them without permission. This directly contradicts the original restriction within the same sentence, creating a clear inconsistency. The contradicted text is the same location as the Changed Text, but the meaning is directly in contradiction with itself.
